items.headline: City Council reviews capital improvement projects for next fiscal year
items.summary: Mountain View is evaluating its Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major infrastructure and development investments. This study session determines what public works projects, facilities upgrades, and community improvements the city will prioritize and fund.

items.headline: New tenant protections ordinance introduced for Sunnyvale renters
items.summary: City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program, signaling potential new rules or expanded protections for renters in Sunnyvale. Details on what specific protections are being added should be monitored as this moves through approval.

items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan gets status update
items.summary: City Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. This ongoing initiative addresses improvements to San José's public library system and services.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus density incentive
items.summary: The City Council is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow the owner an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This is part of the city's effort to preserve historic structures while allowing development.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial to a new planned community district, building an 8-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant residential growth in that corridor and will require City Council approval.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents interested in ongoing construction projects in Los Gatos may want to follow up on what this contract covers.

items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and arts education in the community.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to upgrade traffic signals at the Bascom Avenue highway-rail crossing. This infrastructure project aims to improve safety where vehicle traffic meets railroad crossings.
